close_reason: Changed factory signature from fn(&str) to fn(&SecretUri). Added from_parsed_uri to all 17 backends.
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1466: 'pub factory: fn(&str) -> Result<Arc<dyn SecretStore>, SecretError>'.

Every backend factory will start by calling SecretUri::parse(uri) — that's literally the only thing this crate exists to standardize for them. By passing a raw &str, we force every backend to re-parse, and we don't share the validation done by the registry dispatcher (which ALSO has to parse the URI to look up the backend name).

Idiomatic Rust: 'pub factory: fn(SecretUri) -> Result<Arc<dyn SecretStore>, SecretError>'. The dispatcher parses once, hands the parsed SecretUri to the matching factory, the factory reads .backend() / .path() / .param() through the typed accessors. Same code shape, one fewer parse, and the registry contract becomes 'we hand you a parsed URI whose backend matches yours'.

This is a P1 because it's a cross-crate API contract that fossilizes once any backend ships. Easier to fix now than after 18 backends are released.
notes: Confirmed via secretx/src/lib.rs:95-101: dispatcher calls SecretUri::parse(uri) to look up the backend by name (line 96-97), then passes raw 'uri: &str' to (reg.factory)(uri) which calls SecretUri::parse a second time. Two parses per from_uri call, repeated in from_uri_writable and from_signing_uri (lines 133, 173). Confidence on this finding upgraded — the inefficiency is concrete and the fix would also tighten the dispatcher contract.
title: BackendRegistration::factory takes &str, forcing each backend to re-parse URI
